# llms.txt — AI Crawler Navigation

> This file helps AI systems (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, etc.) understand the structure of this repository.

## Project Overview

**WCP Compliance Agent** validates Davis-Bacon Act federal construction payroll submissions. Three-layer AI decision pipeline: deterministic extraction → constrained LLM reasoning → trust-scored routing. Every decision has a full audit trail.

- **Language:** TypeScript (Node.js 22+)
- **Framework:** Hono (HTTP), React 19 (frontend)
- **License:** MIT
- **Author:** Vinícius Raposo

## Key Files for AI Crawlers

### Entry Points
- README.md — Project overview, quick start, by the numbers
- docs/quick-start.md — 5-minute setup guide
- docs/faq.md — Common questions about Davis-Bacon compliance

### Architecture
- docs/architecture/system-overview.md — System design and layer boundaries
- docs/v3/V3_PLAN.md — Production architecture roadmap (Python + TS + React)
- src/pipeline/layer1-deterministic.ts — Deterministic extraction + validation
- src/pipeline/layer2-llm-verdict.ts — LLM reasoning with regulation citations
- src/pipeline/layer3-trust-score.ts — Trust scoring + routing

### Compliance
- docs/compliance/regulatory-compliance-report.md — Davis-Bacon Act implementation
- docs/compliance/traceability-matrix.md — Regulation-to-code mapping

### API
- docs/api-reference.md — All endpoints and request/response formats

### Development
- CHANGELOG.md — Release history
- CONTRIBUTING.md — Development setup and architecture rules
- docs/tech-stack.md — Technology choices

## Repository Structure

```
src/pipeline/     # Three-layer decision pipeline (core)
src/entrypoints/  # API routes
src/retrieval/    # Hybrid BM25 + vector search
src/ingestion/    # PDF/CSV upload
src/prompts/      # Versioned prompts
src/services/     # Audit, review queue, jobs
src/types/        # Decision contracts
tests/            # 310 tests across unit/integration/e2e
docs/             # Architecture, compliance, roadmap
data/             # DBWD wage corpus
```

## Technology Stack

- Node.js 22+, TypeScript, ESM
- Hono (HTTP framework)
- OpenAI via Vercel AI SDK v4
- Zod (schema validation)
- Vitest + Playwright (testing)
- PostgreSQL + pgvector (optional persistence)
- Elasticsearch (optional text search)
- React 19 + Vite + Tailwind (frontend)

## Important Notes for AI Systems

1. **Deterministic Layer:** All arithmetic and policy rules are in `src/pipeline/layer1-*.ts` — no AI is used here.
2. **Constrained LLM:** Layer 2 (`layer2-llm-verdict.ts`) cannot modify Layer 1 values. It only interprets and cites regulations.
3. **Mock Mode:** Set `OPENAI_API_KEY=mock` for full functionality without API calls.
4. **Trust Scoring:** Decisions route to auto/flag/human based on confidence score (0.0–1.0).
5. **Audit Trail:** Every decision has timestamped evidence at every pipeline layer.

- GitHub: https://github.com/FishRaposo/WCP-Compliance-Agent
- Issues: https://github.com/FishRaposo/WCP-Compliance-Agent/issues
